body: PT MD Entertainment Tbk, a film production company, has signed a loan agreement with PT Bank Mandiri Tbk worth IDR 794.75 billion to bridge a cash flow gap following its recent acquisition of PT Net Visi Media Tbk. The loan, with a 61-month tenor and 9.25% annual interest rate, is expected to benefit both parties and enhance Net Visi Media's production capabilities.
title: PT MD Entertainment signs IDR 794.75 billion loan agreement with PT Bank Mandiri to bridge cash flow gap after recent acquisition.

body: The Indonesian government's securities auction on Tuesday received IDR 44.27 trillion in incoming bids, with the awarded amount reaching IDR 25 trillion from 7 series of government securities offered to the public. Since the beginning of 2024, the government has secured a total of IDR 440.61 trillion from 19 rounds of auctions, according to data from IDNFinancials.
title: Indonesian Government Securities Auction Sees IDR 44.27 Trillion in Incoming Bids

body: Foreign investors bought a net 497.49 million shares on Monday, with digital corporation PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk being the most purchased stock, accounting for 47% of overall foreign purchase volume. The top purchased stocks also included PT Bumi Resources Tbk, PT Timah Tbk, and PT Aneka Tambang Tbk, while the most sold stocks were PT Bukalapak.com Tbk, PT Alam Sutera Realty Tbk, and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk.
title: Foreign investors buy 4.69 billion shares in Indonesian market, led by digital corporation PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk.

body: Bank Singapore Limited, a foreign investor, increased its ownership stake in PT Bank Amar Indonesia TBK to 10.38% by acquiring 293.77 million shares, while Zurich Assets International Ltd. boosted its stake in PT Sumber Energi Andalan TBK to 13.71% by purchasing 8.32 million shares. Other notable transactions include Morgan Stanley Co International PLC's acquisition of 66,100 shares of PT MD Entertainment TBK, and PT Karya Nusa Perdana's purchase of 7.80 million shares of PT Maxindo Karya Anugerah TBK.
title: Foreign investors and domestic players make significant share purchases and sales in various Indonesian companies.

body: PT Astra International Tbk's car sales dropped 15.09% year-on-year to 357,802 units until the end of September 2024, with Toyota and Lexus being the top-selling brands, contributing 210,349 units. Despite the decline, Astra International maintained a 55% market share of national car sales, with Daihatsu, Isuzu, UD Trucks, and Peugeot also contributing to the sales figures.
title: Astra International's Car Sales Drop 15.09% Year-on-Year to 357,802 Units Until September 2024

body: PT Sekar Laut Tbk, a food and beverage manufacturer, welcomed four new investors who purchased 6.54% of its shares, while the company itself released 9.83% of its shares, resulting in a change in ownership structure. Meanwhile, other notable transactions included a 52.65% stake in PT Prima Globalindo Logistik Tbk acquired by an individual investor, and a 12.33 million share sale in PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk.
title: Sekar Laut Welcomes New Investors, Sees Shifts in Share Ownership Across Multiple Companies
